questions: You're not easily grossed out by anything. You open your fridge and find a container of leftovers from three weeks ago covered in fuzzy green mold. What do you do?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You're cleaning out a clogged drain and pull out a thick wad of hair mixed with soap scum. How do you handle it?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You walk into a public restroom at a highway rest stop and it clearly hasn't been cleaned in days. What's your reaction?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. A friend shows you a video of a medical procedure where they're draining a large abscess. How do you respond?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You're at a restaurant and notice a cockroach crawling across the floor near your table. What do you think?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You're helping a friend move and discover a pile of old, damp towels behind their washing machine that smell strongly of mildew. How do you react?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. Your dog rolls in something dead at the park and comes running over to you, reeking. What do you do?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You bite into a piece of fruit and see half a worm in the part you just ate. What goes through your mind?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You read about a company that was caught selling meat products with unsanitary processing conditions. How does that make you feel?
You're not easily grossed out by anything. You're volunteering at an animal shelter and have to clean out kennels with waste that's been sitting since yesterday morning. How do you approach it?
